evidence_rea: Provide documentation to customers which describes the system's: 2) evidence that the system is fit for purpose for each intended use.
evidence_reg: (iv) where applicable, The Provider shall provide information that is relevant to explain its output; (v) when appropriate, The Provider shall perform regarding specific persons or groups of persons on which the system is intended to be used;
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA addresses documentation about fitness for purpose for intended uses, while the REG text concerns information explaining output and another unclear clause about specific persons or groups; these do not clearly express the same constraint, so no quote-supported mismatch can be established.

evidence_rea: Provide documentation to customers which describes the system's: 2) evidence that the system is fit for purpose for each intended use.
evidence_reg: The instructions for use shall contain least the following information: (i) its intended purpose;
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA refers to providing documentation describing evidence that the system is fit for purpose for each intended use, while the REG requires instructions for use to contain information on intended purpose; the texts are related but not directly conflicting, and omission alone is not a deviation.

evidence_rea: Provide documentation to customers which describes the system's: 2) evidence that the system is fit for purpose for each intended use.
evidence_reg: The Provider SHALL draw up a single set of technical documentation containing all the information set out in paragraph 1, as well as the information required under those legal acts where a high-risk ai system related to a product covered by the union harmonisation legislation listed in section a of annex i is placed on the market or put into service.
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA concerns providing documentation to customers, whereas the REG concerns drawing up a single set of technical documentation under specified conditions; these are different obligations and no direct conflict is clearly stated.
